posts.text:
This is genuinely inexcusable. Everyone expected this. People across the political spectrum screamed from the rooftops about this possibility. So Trump is either lying or so insulated from reliable information he didn’t know this would happen. Two extremely bad possibilities.


Acyn (@Acyn)

Trump: They weren't supposed to go after all these other countries in the Middle East. So they hit Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait. Nobody expected that. We were shocked

Video
  





— https://ni
Oh, also: it’s going to cost $1.2 billion. usaspending.gov/award/CONT_A…

People often say "this is not a partisan issue" about issues that are, actually, partisan. But in Texas, this one isn't. Anyone who has ever been to Big Bend knows you don't need a wall, especially not in this area. We are about to lose something unbelievably special for NO gain

This is all about to happen despite the fact a bipartisan group of sheriffs, every local Republican from Hudspeth down to Brewster county, and even the most MAGA & pro-Trump voters in the area oppose it. Only @GregAbbott_TX or @SenMullin can stop it now nitter.net/WestTxStringer/status/…


Matt Stringer (@WestTxStringer)

New: A bipartisan coalition of Texas border sheriffs sign a joint letter of opposition to the construction of a border wall in Big Bend National Park, and are urging federa

So now... the wall is happening, and the most rugged, pristine lands in the lower 48 are about to be destroyed. Along with my family's river and horseback business, and thousands of other jobs in this unbelievably special place.  readtangle.com/trump-border-…

A lot of people saw this and got complacent. But the latest news is that a $1 billion contract has been awarded in Brewster County, which is home to the National Park. For steel bollard walls. nitter.net/lukemetzger/status/202…


Luke Metzger (@lukemetzger)

Updated map of border wall on Border Patrol website now shows CBP plans to use detection technology instead of a physical wall through Big Bend!





— https://nitter.net/lukemetzger/status/2029693089774702988#m

It's hard to explain how self-defeating this is. The Big Bend sector is ~25% of the entire border with Mexico, and ~1% of all border crossings. And literally hundreds a year happen in the areas around the National Park. A wall will cut Texans off from the most important natural resource in West Texas, the river, and hand it over to Mexico

With all eyes focused on Iran, Trump and DHS are pushing forward on border wall construction through Big Bend National Park. They will hand the Rio Grande to Mexico, destroy thousands of jobs, and ruin the last remaining wild West, all for less than 1% of all border crossings:
I deleted this tweet because the CNN "clarification" is so obviously an indication they flubbed the story. They should have just retracted it.

I spoke to @DavidAFrench ab the “volcanic” blowback to his James Talarico piece, whether he’d vote for him if he lived in Texas, the legality of this war in Iran (domestically and under international law), and what it’s like to work at The New York Times. Links to watch & read👇
